title: toona ciliata var. multijuga
intro: Toona ciliata var. multijuga, also known as the Bael or Bengal Bael, is a variety of the Toona ciliata tree, which belongs to the family Meliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is also valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to aid digestion and reduce fever. The leaves and bark of the Toona ciliata var. multijuga plant are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin problems. The plant's medicinal properties have also been found to have antioxidant and antiseptic effects, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
